title: 2026 04 07 HackerNews
content: Google released an offline iPhone app that runs Gemma 4 LLMs locally for privacy and edge‑AI. A tiny 8‑million‑parameter model, GuppyLM, demonstrates core LLM mechanics in a browser. Commentary criticizes forced app downloads, Microsoft’s disjointed GUI strategy, and broad UI rule violations. Swiss fiber surpasses U.S. and German broadband due to shared infrastructure. Claude Code’s February updates reduced reasoning depth, causing performance drops. France moved remaining gold out of the U.S., earning €13 bn gains. Employers now use personal data to set pay floors. Local LLM hosting tools (LM Studio, LM Studio CLI) and MoE models enable efficient on‑device inference.

title: 停止和大模型聊天：产品经理的AI工作流重构指南
content: The article explains why most product managers still use AI as a simple chat tool, limiting productivity. It introduces “Agentic Workflow,” which turns AI into an autonomous, self‑correcting executor, seamlessly accesses project context, and builds reusable assets. Three key shifts are: moving from manual feedback loops to closed‑loop execution, from limited prompts to continuous context sharing, and from consumptive usage to investment‑style asset accumulation. It provides a concrete case of post‑launch failure analysis, showing how structured local files and AI tools like Cursor enable end‑to‑end insight generation, turning PMs into strategic architects rather than just executioners.

title: 美军特种兵潜入伊朗救人场面失控，运输机「失灵」了，为什么会出现这种情况？暴露出美军的哪些短板？
content: A U.S. special‑forces raid in Iran went awry when two MC‑130 transport planes failed to lift off, forcing the use of smaller aircraft to evacuate a wounded F‑15E weapons officer who had hid on a mountain ridge. U.S. troops interfered with Iranian electronics, bombed surrounding roads, and destroyed the abandoned transport aircraft to prevent capture. Iranian officials claim two black‑hawk helicopters and two C‑130s were shot down, while the U.S. maintains only mechanical failure. This incident highlights logistical and operational vulnerabilities in U.S. air operations.

title: 真正的一手战地情报：霍尔木兹海峡实地调研 | 原文
content: The article reports on a firsthand investigation of the Strait of Hormuz, a key energy corridor with limited real‑time visibility. An analyst (designer “No. 3”) was sent by the Sitorisidi Research Firm to assess whether the strait remains open or closed. During the trip, the analyst faced restrictions at the Oman border, covertly transported equipment, and encountered stringent protocols in Musandam. The mission revealed significant gaps in public data and underscored the challenges of monitoring such a strategically critical location.

title: 老挝：一场“被遗忘的战争”与绵延不散的伤痕 | 原文
content: Much of Laos remains scarred by a war that is often called “forgotten.”  In Vientiane’s COPE visitor centre, displays show that bombing stopped, yet buried mines, amputations and the psychological trauma they cause persist.  Photographs depict a severed leg with bone exposed, a young man laughing after a leg amputation, and other survivors.  The centre is a reminder that war‑injuries still haunt the country’s population and landscape.

title: 交易成功的核心要素：顺势、止损、规则、执行力
content: The core of successful trading is following the market trend, setting well‑defined stop‑losses, operating under strict rules, and executing them with discipline. Traders must determine a suitable trend‑analysis period, use simple, clear reversal criteria, and trade only when conditions are met—avoiding sideways markets. Stop‑losses convert probability advantage into certainty, preventing big losses that can wipe out many small wins. Rules limit emotional, impulsive trading, and disciplined execution ensures the plan actually works.
source: 热帖 - 雪球 - 鑫鑫-投资

title: GitHub Copilot CLI combines model families for a second opinion
content: GitHub Copilot CLI now offers “Rubber Duck,” a second‑model reviewer that checks plans, code, and tests before execution. Pairing Claude Sonnet with GPT‑5.4 closes 74.7 % of the performance gap between Sonnet and Opus on difficult, multi‑file tasks. Rubber Duck reviews key checkpoints—after planning, complex implementation, or before test run—and can be called on demand. It finds architectural flaws, logical bugs, and hidden edge cases, improving accuracy for high‑stakes code generation.

title: 伊朗拒绝临时停火强调永久停战，并提出十项要求，主要想实现什么？伊方强硬立场下，谈判破裂对哪一方更不利？
content: Iran rejected a temporary ceasefire offered by the United States, insisting that any pause must be a permanent end to the conflict. Tehran’s stance includes ten demands: permanent cessation of hostilities, war reparations, a security agreement for the Strait of Hormuz, postwar reconstruction, full lifting of sanctions, withdrawal of U.S. forces, and Iran’s pledge not to pursue nuclear weapons in exchange for sanctions relief. Pakistani mediation plans a staged ceasefire, but Iran’s hard line risks prolonging negotiations, disadvantaging both sides by delaying resolution.

title: Connecting MCP servers to Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Gateway using Authorization Code flow
content: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Gateway centralizes authentication, observability, and policy enforcement for AI agents connecting to multiple MCP servers. It supports OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code flow via Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Identity, allowing agents to access protected servers without embedding credentials. Admin users can set up MCP targets either by completing the OAuth flow during target creation (implicit sync) or by providing tool schemas upfront. Users listing tools use cached definitions, while invoking tools triggers the OAuth flow, which is secured with session‑binding to prevent hijacking.

title: 美国250岁生日，《经济学人》“英式毒蛇”辣评（二） | 原文
content: The Economist’s “America’s 250th Birthday” series critiques how free‑market expansion spawned its own critics. The second installment examines the 1870‑1910 period—robber barons, muckraking journalists, sweatshops, and progressive reforms. It analyzes anti‑trust actions, xenophobia, racial segregation, and imperial ambition, revealing America’s first real “great‑power anxiety.” In the clash between liberty, power, capital, and justice, early seeds of today’s controversies are traced.

title: Google Maps for Codebases: Paste a GitHub URL, Ask Anything
content: Codebase Navigator lets users paste a GitHub URL and ask plain‑English questions about the repo. It builds a real dependency graph from imports, highlights relevant lines, shows the file tree, and updates a code viewer—all in real time. Built with Next.js, CopilotKit, Octokit, React Flow, and Ollama (or OpenAI) for LLM queries, it uses local caching, Zustand for state, and tool‑based LLM interactions to avoid hallucinations and keep everything in sync.

title: Age Verification as Mass Surveillance Infrastructure

The article examines how such verification infrastructures expand surveillance capabilities beyond their original purpose.

title: 一日一撸码（No.3）： claude code provider 快速切换启动器（for CC switch users）
content: CCSC is a tool for Claude Switch users who need to use multiple Claude service providers (Anthropic, local models, Coding plans). It avoids the default method of editing ~/.claude/settings.json, which changes global settings and affects currently running sessions. Instead, CCSC isolates each session by launching new Claude processes, leaving global config untouched, and lets each terminal use a different provider. It integrates with existing CC Switch configurations, making it useful for developing projects with different providers or testing a code base across models.

content: Obsidian, a local‑first Markdown note‑taking app valued at $350 million, runs on a core team of just three full‑time engineers and a handful of staff. It thrives by rejecting venture capital, relying on paid add‑ons and an open‑source plugin ecosystem that lets users extend it freely. The company emphasizes zero meetings, minimal management, and a “Ramblings” private‑channel culture to maintain alignment. Its philosophy contrasts with Notion’s cloud‑centric, data‑controlled model, offering users complete local data ownership and privacy.

title: 30 秒自测--快速鉴定 Claude 模型真伪
content: A quick self‑check for Claude Opus 4.6 uses three tests: ask the model its exact name (Opus 4.6 will specify “Claude Opus 4.6”); copy a sentence with Chinese quotes and verify that the output uses straight quotation marks—real Opus changes Chinese quotes, fakes keep the original curves; and submit a Japanese‑name prompt that forces multiple languages; real Opus shows garbled text (Mojibake) and a “stress‑test” pattern, while fakes produce clean output. Combine results for a reliable assessment.

content: You maintain a large Java project with 15,000 classes and use JRebel for hot deployment. Your leaders provide verbal requirements, and you are exploring how to integrate Claude Code (cc). After cc’s changes, you compile via Maven configured in Idea, but you encounter path, whitespace, and other errors, leading to a poor experience. The question is how to properly use cc for Java development—whether cc should modify code, use Idea’s hot deployment, or handle errors differently.

In a data‑rich era, on‑site research is invaluable. Citrini Research’s analyst visited the Strait of Hormuz and found that labeling the waterway as fully open or closed is misleading. Military tension and commercial traffic coexist, so simple escalation models fail. Public AIS data underestimates traffic by at least 50%; Greek oil tankers and LPG ships are still traversing the center. title: 大学专业死亡潮
content: University programs in China are being cut at a record pace—Sichuan University reduced 39 undergraduate majors, and other institutions have similarly eliminated dozens of art, language and engineering titles. Reasons cited include excess supply, poor job prospects and the rise of AI, which is reshaping skill needs. In response, new programs in artificial intelligence, digital economy, robotics, and interdisciplinary fields are being created, reflecting a national shift toward future-oriented talent development.

title: Employers use your personal data to figure out the lowest salary you'll accept
content: Employers analyze personal data to determine the minimum salary candidates will accept. By reviewing online profiles, past salary, educational background, and other publicly available information, recruiters estimate a target compensation window. This approach helps them bid precisely, potentially lowering pay offers while still attracting candidates. It raises concerns about fairness and privacy, as job seekers may feel pressured to accept lower wages based on data-driven profiling rather than negotiated value.

title: 【读书】【互联网】多样性是社区繁荣的核心要素？——读《美国大城市的死与生》 | 原文
content: The article discusses how Jane Jacobs’ book *The Death and Life of Great American Cities* offers lessons for internet community platforms. It treats platforms as city planners, emphasizing that a diverse, active community—where everyone contributes—ensures a thriving ecosystem. Jacobs’ critique of large-scale urban planning is applied to online spaces, suggesting that varied participation and thoughtful design are essential for sustainable community growth.

title: 妈妈不知儿子去世跟AI儿子聊了1年，AI「复活」逝者的服务有伦理风险吗？如果是你，会更愿意知道真相吗？
content: A Chinese entrepreneur, Zhang Zewei, uses generative AI to create digital replicas of deceased relatives. Clients employ these AI “children” to comfort grieving families, letting grandparents interact with a simulated son, or helping children recover from depression after a father’s death. Zhang questions whether the recreated person is truly the deceased or merely a sentimental projection. He cites a film line, suggesting that the true suffering may be inflicted on the living rather than the departed.

title: 黄仁勋最新访谈：要想成事，这4点远比智力更重要
content: NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang argues that in the AI era intelligence will become a cheap commodity, making IQ less valuable than human traits. He identifies four irreplaceable human capabilities: resilience and the ability to forget pain, empathy and trust, a sense of purpose and vision, and humility with a growth mindset. Huang advises people to focus on non‑standardized, human‑value tasks, act with resilience, build trust instead of contracts, and maintain a learner’s attitude.

title: 下一个十年，决定你贫富的，不再是努力，而是它
content: Token revolution turns every unit of human insight into a quantifiable “token” that can be instantly evaluated, traded, and replicated by AI. Unlike past freedoms—mechanization and information—this shift liberates the output of knowledge itself, allowing AI to generate millions of high‑value tokens daily. Industries such as finance, media, and healthcare are reshaped as AI‑driven token creation supersedes human expertise, concentrating wealth among those who define, manage, and circulate tokens, while ordinary labor risks obsolescence.

title: 特朗普是那位将亚洲拱手让给中国的总统吗？
content: The article examines claims that former President Trump “gave up” Asia to China, questioning whether his administration's policies effectively ceded influence in the region. It reviews Trump’s diplomatic actions, trade deals, and strategic moves, contrasting them with prior U.S. engagements. The piece suggests that, rather than relinquishing Asia, Trump’s policies shifted focus and tactics, and the notion of a complete handover oversimplifies complex regional dynamics.

title: “合法的”庞氏骗局？揭秘 Gemini 交易所与其创始人的循环借贷 | 原文
content: Gemini’s 10‑K filings reveal a looped lending scheme: the company’s founder farms crypto through his own company, WCF, which lends it to Gemini. Gemini then uses those crypto assets as collateral to obtain USD loans from a third party, creating internal circular borrowing. This arrangement mimics a Ponzi‑style structure, with the founder effectively financing Gemini’s operations from his own assets. The piece warns readers that such practices are neither transparent nor legally sound.

title: 你的新工作是引导智能体入职：探索AI原生公司的运作机制
content: The article explains that in AI-native companies, guiding and managing AI agents is core work. Linear treats agents as full‑time team members, automating task creation, specification, and coding through tools like Codex and Claude Code. Ramp promotes AI fluency across roles with a four‑level framework and measures usage to drive adoption. Factory builds its organization around agents from day one, using “skills” written in markdown to encode expert knowledge and ensure agent‑ready codebases. Together, they show how to onboard agents as employees, measure proficiency, and embed skills for organizational efficiency.
